figure.title: Receive Catch Buffers
figure.purpose: Details the architecture of the catch buffers used in the TL receive path to hold parsed flit sectors before queuing.

technical_summary: An Inbound Receive (Rx) TL Flit (64 Bytes) enters the TL Flit Decoder.
Decoded request data goes to a 4-entry Req Catch Buffer (each entry is 4 sectors wide). The buffer shifts one entry per TL flit.
Decoded response data goes to an 8-entry Rsp Catch Buffer (each entry is 2 sectors wide). This buffer also shifts one entry per TL flit.
Data from the Req Catch Buffer feeds into the Rx Req Queue and the Rx Address Cache/Address CAM.
Data from the Rsp Catch Buffer feeds into the Rx Rsp Queue.
Outputs from the queues go to the UPLI interface (Requests, Wr Rsp, Rd Rsp).

components.name: Req Catch Buffer
components.description: 4-entry buffer (4-sector width) for temporary storage of incoming requests.

components.name: Rsp Catch Buffer
components.description: 8-entry buffer (2-sector width) for temporary storage of incoming responses.

components.name: Address CAM
components.description: Content Addressable Memory used alongside the Rx Address Cache for address lookup.

ordered_flow: Flit Decoder -> Catch Buffers -> Queues -> External Interfaces

critical_facts: Catch buffers act as an intermediate staging area between the fast flit decoder and the deeper receive queues.
